entries.verdict_line: US 10Y at 4.55% — an 8bps move in 24 hours — while S&P sits at an all-time high and VIX collapses to 18.9; the bond market is pricing Fed tightening that equities are refusing to believe.
PMI Services at 57.5 — an 88th-percentile read — while airline passengers hit a 10-year low at 35mn; India's services expansion is statistical, not experiential.
SIP AUM up 64% YoY while accounts grew only 15% — affluent money is colonising retail mutual funds, and the mass investor the bulls are counting on hasn't arrived.
Copper up 2.4% to $6.40 while iron ore surged 59% — industrial metals are pricing a China recovery that China's own 4.5% GDP and fading PMI flatly contradict.
Airline passengers at a 10-year low of 35mn while GST hits ₹2.01 lakh crore — India's formal economy is booming and its urban consumer is quietly retreating.
Nikkei at an all-time high of 66,020 while US GDP printed 0.7% SAAR — global equities are pricing a soft landing that the world's largest economy is not delivering.

Bank Nifty at a 10-year high of 56,815 while the CD ratio sits at 83% and corporate bond issuance hits a 7th-percentile panic floor — the equity market is pricing banking health that the funding structure does not support.

Iron ore at $161.91/tonne — a 21% single-period surge — while e-commerce GMV growth halved to 12.4% and airline traffic fell 9.1%; the commodity market is pricing a demand boom that India's own consumers are not delivering.
S&P 500 at an all-time high of 7,511 while US GDP prints 0.7% SAAR — global equity markets are pricing a recovery that the world's largest economy has already stopped delivering.
Natural gas at a decade-low ₹3.16 while US core PCE ticks up to 3.1% — the Fed's inflation problem and the world's energy glut are pulling in opposite directions, and one of them is wrong.
SIP inflows fell sequentially for the first time in 8 months to ₹29,845 cr — even as MF AUM hit ₹82 lakh crore; retail is still in, but the marginal buyer is quietly stepping back.

US 10-year at 4.49% — an 81st-percentile print — while the Fed holds at 3.625% and core PCE ticks to 3.1%; the carry trade that funded EM inflows is repricing, and India's ₹22,729 cr FII swing in a single month is the first casualty.

Airline passenger traffic fell 8.5% MoM to 35 million — a 7th-percentile print in non-seasonal March — while mass-market GST and auto sales surge; India's affluent consumer is quietly stepping off the escalator.
Nikkei at a 10-year high while US GDP prints 0.7% SAAR and India's PMI composite slips 240bps — global equity is pricing a growth acceleration that no major economy's output data is delivering.
India VIX at an 8th-percentile 12.84 while Bank Nifty hits a 96th-percentile high — the market is pricing perfection into banks whose deposit tanks are running 300bps behind their credit engines.
Copper down 3.6% to $6.13/lb while iron ore surges 60% — industrial metals are sending contradictory signals, and one of them is lying about the global growth cycle.
S&P 500 at an all-time high yesterday, down 1.5% today — global equity is pricing perfection while US GDP prints 0.7% SAAR and every major index rolls over simultaneously.
Natural gas up 1.4% to $3.27 while copper surges 3% to $6.12 — industrial inputs are repricing before India's fuel deflation turns, and the INR at 94.34 has no cushion when the lag closes.

SIP accounts at a decade-low 2nd percentile while AUM hits ₹16.64 tr — India's mutual fund engine is concentrating into fewer, wealthier hands, and the retail democratisation story is quietly reversing.
US 10Y at 4.4% — a 79th-percentile print falling while core PCE ticks up to 3.1% — the bond market is pricing Fed cuts that the Fed's own inflation data won't permit.

Airline passenger traffic at a 7th-percentile 35.08 million while vehicle sales surge 26–29% — India's consumption story is bifurcating, and the segment that can't be faked by GST formalization is pointing down.
Nikkei at a 100th-percentile all-time high while US GDP prints 0.7% SAAR — global equities are levitating on liquidity, not growth, and the landing will not be soft.
Euro Stoxx 50 and S&P 500 both at 100th-percentile highs while US GDP prints 0.7% SAAR — global equities are pricing a recovery that no growth data has yet authorised.
US 10Y at 4.44% — an 80th-percentile yield rising while US GDP prints 0.7% SAAR — the bond market is tightening what the Fed refuses to, and India's ₹95.24 INR has no rate defence.
India VIX at a 7th-percentile 12.29 while Bank Nifty flatlines at a 96th-percentile high — the market is pricing perfection into a credit system where deposits are losing the race to loans by 300bps.

Copper at $6.224 — a 73rd-percentile print rising while US GDP prints 0.7% SAAR — the metal is pricing a China stimulus bounce, not a global recovery, and India's capex narrative is borrowing someone else's signal.

SIP accounts growing at a 2nd-percentile crawl while AUM hits 82nd-percentile highs — retail India is deepening existing wallets, not widening the base, and that distinction matters for the next market correction.
Iron ore at $161.91 — a 65% single-session surge to a 63rd-percentile print — is either the most important China stimulus signal of 2026 or the most dangerous head-fake for India's capex narrative.
Airline passengers down 9.1% MoM to a 7th-percentile low while GST prints ₹2.01 lakh crore — India's consumption story has two floors, and the one carrying the weight just cracked.
SIP inflows slipped to ₹29,845 cr while FII equity net flipped to -₹114 cr — domestic liquidity is still the floor, but it just got thinner.
